slides.title: AI-Native Enterprise Mini MBA Lab
slides.subtitle: Welcome — Day 1
slides.body: Over the next 10 sessions we will build the mindset, frameworks and hands-on skills to lead AI transformation inside your organisation.

slides.title: What is an AI-native enterprise?
slides.body: An AI-native company embeds intelligence across every layer of its operations — not as a tool bolted on, but as the operating system of the business. Decisions are informed by models, workflows are automated, and humans focus on judgment and creativity.
slides.facilitator_notes: Spend ~5 min here. Ask the group: 'Can anyone name a company that feels AI-native vs one that just bolted AI on?' Draw out the difference between using AI as a tool vs as the operating model. Don't rush — this framing anchors the whole course.

slides.title: The AI maturity spectrum
slides.body: Organisations move through four stages: digitised → data-driven → AI-enabled → AI-native. Most enterprises today sit between stage 2 and 3.

slides.title: Three hallmarks of AI-native orgs
points: Data infrastructure is a strategic asset, not an afterthought
AI is embedded in decision loops, not just dashboards
Employees are trained to work with — and direct — AI systems

slides.title: Executive insight
slides.quote: The question is no longer whether to use AI, but how fast you can rewire your operating model around it.

slides.title: AI adoption maturity — your organisation
slides.description: Rate your organisation in each area (0–10)

slides.title: AI opportunity canvas
slides.description: Fill in the table for your department

slides.title: Day 1 wrap-up
slides.body: Today we covered the foundations of AI-native thinking. Tomorrow we will move into AI Strategy and mapping opportunities across your value chain.

activities.title: Knowledge check — AI-native fundamentals
activities.question: Which of the following best describes an AI-native organisation?
options: One that uses AI only in its IT department
An organisation where AI is embedded across all decision-making layers
A company that has adopted cloud computing
A business that sells AI products

activities.title: Knowledge check — AI strategy
activities.question: What is the first step in building an AI opportunity map?
options: Identifying high-value, high-feasibility use cases across the value chain

items.text: AI-native companies treat data as a strategic ___
